steps.title: Solve the equation
steps.content: The right side equals $22^2$, so $$x^2=22^2.$$ Taking square roots gives $x=\pm 22$.

steps.title: Identify the positive solution
steps.content: Take the positive root. The answer is 22.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: The correct answer is $22$. The given equation, ${x}^{2}=\left(22\right)\left(22\right)$, is equivalent to ${x}^{2}={\left(22\right)}^{2}$. Taking the square root of each side of this equation yields $x=\pm22$. Thus, the positive solution to the given equation is $22$.
